nodes.title: CLIP Text Encode (Positive Prompt)
widgets_values: A sweaty, determined woman in a black racing suit and helmet drives a race car, her hands on the steering wheel, speaking in Spanish saying: "Box, box, vuelta ahora. Una ventaja ya en la entrada, Cambio."

# How to Prompt a Good Lipdub/Rephrase

This workflow supports both **dubbing** (translating speech into another language) and **rephrasing** (changing what the speaker says while keeping the same language). In both cases, the model will generate new lip movements and audio to match the text you provide.

## 1. Include the translated words in your prompt

The prompt should contain the translated words you want the speaker to say. The model will **not** translate it for you.

## 2. Use native script

Use the alphabet of the language you want the model to speak - for example, Cyrillic for Russian or Chinese characters for Chinese.

## 3. Single speaker only

The current LoRA supports only one speaker.

## 4. Match the length of the original for the best audio

Try to keep your translation close to the length of the original dialogue.

- **Too long** — The model might skip words.
- **Too short** — The output might sound slow and unnatural.

> **Tip:** Since there are many ways to say the same thing, play around with your wording until the length is right.

The `latent_downscale_factor` output from the IC-LoRA Loader is intentionally ignored. The lipdub LoRA was trained with `reference_downscale_factor = 1` (full resolution), so no spatial downscaling of the reference is needed. For reference on handling other values, see `LTX-2.3_ICLoRA_Union_Control_Distilled.json`
